'''Sauaso "Jesse" Sue''' (born 20 April 1992) is a [[Samoa national rugby league team|Samoan]] international [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|pr|lf}} and {{rlp|sr}} forward for [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] in the [[Super League]].

He previously played for the [[Wests Tigers]], [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs]] and [[Newcastle Knights]] in the [[National Rugby League]].

==Early years==
Sue was born in [[Ōtāhuhu]], [[New Zealand]], and is of [[Samoan people|Samoan]] descent.

Sue moved to Australia as a 2-year old and attended [[James Meehan High School]] located in [[Sydney]]'s south west. Sue played his junior football for the Macquarie Fields Hawks before being signed by the [[Wests Tigers]].{{citation needed|date=October 2022}}

Sue played for the Wests Tigers [[National Youth Competition (rugby league)|NYC]] team in 2011 and 2012,<ref>{{cite web|url=http://rleague.com/players/Sauaso-Sue_3143/Matches/|title=Sauaso Sue player results, scores, news and statistics|publisher=Rleague.com|date=1992-04-20|access-date=8 September 2013}}</ref> and was a member of the team that won the grand final in 2012.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/wests-tigers-claim-maiden-toyota-cup-premieship-with-46-6-victory-over-canberra-at-anz-stadium/story-e6frf3ou-1226484801535|title=Wests Tigers claim maiden Toyota Cup premiership with 46-6 victory over Canberra at ANZ Stadium|author=Joe Barton|work=Fox Sports|date=|access-date=2016-03-06}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.heraldsun.com.au/sport/nrl/wests-tigers-thrash-canberra-raides-to-take-toyota-cup-title/story-e6frfgbo-1226484807862|title=Wests Tigers thrash Canberra Raiders to take Toyota Cup title|publisher=Heraldsun.com.au|date=2012-09-12|access-date=2016-03-06}}</ref> In October 2012, Sue played for the [[Junior Kiwis]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nzrl.co.nz/news-archive/junior-kiwis-team-to-play-saturday-named.aspx|title=NZRL|publisher=NZRL|date=10 October 2012|access-date=8 March 2015}}</ref>

==Playing career==
===2013===
In Round 6 of the [[2013 NRL season]] Sue made his [[NRL]] debut for the [[Wests Tigers]] against the [[St. George Illawarra Dragons]] off the interchange bench in the Tigers 13-12 loss at [[Sydney Cricket Ground|SCG]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/jamie-soward-field-goal-for-st-george-illawarra-steals-victory-against-wests-tigers/story-fn2mcuj6-1226620187886|title=Jamie Soward field goal for St George Illawarra steals victory against Wests Tigers|work=Fox Sports|date=|access-date=2016-03-06}}</ref> He was a regular in first grade for the rest of the season, playing in 17 games, and was one of the Wests Tigers most effective defenders.<ref name="young guns">{{cite news|author=Brock Corfe|work=weststigers.com.au|title=Young guns - Sauaso Sue|url=http://www.weststigers.com.au/news-display/Young-guns--Sauaso-Sue/82447|access-date=15 September 2013|date=20 August 2013}}</ref> Coach [[Mick Potter]] said, "His defence is outstanding but what is even more impressive is that he wins the tackles. He doesn't get beaten on the ground. As well as being aggressive he dominates his tackles and that comes from his leg-drive and his strength." In Round 14 against the [[Brisbane Broncos]], Sue scored his first NRL career, and only season, try.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/brisbane-broncos-return-to-winners-circle-with-32-12-win-over-wests-tigers-at-suncorp-stadium/story-fn2mcuj6-1226665283715|title=Brisbane Broncos return to winner's circle with 32-12 win over Wests Tigers at Suncorp Stadium|author=Laine Clark|work=Fox Sports|date=|access-date=2016-03-06}}</ref>
[[File:sauasosue.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Jesse Sue playing for the Tigers in 2013]]
At the end of the regular season, Sue was selected at international level for the [[Samoa national rugby league team|Samoans]] in the [[2013 Rugby League World Cup|2013 World Cup]]. Sue appeared in all 4 games that the Samoans played at the tournament, scoring a try against [[Papua New Guinea national rugby team|Papua New Guinea]].

===2014===
Sue played in 18 matches for the season. In September, he was selected for the [[New Zealand national rugby league team]], and Samoa [[2014 Rugby League Four Nations|Four Nations]] train-on squads.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.radionz.co.nz/news/sport/254707/six-players-added-to-kiwis-squad|title=Six players added to Kiwis squad|publisher=Radionz.co.nz|date=19 September 2014|access-date=8 March 2015}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.zerotackle.com/nrl/samao-train-on-squad-announced-14837/|title=Samoa train on squad announced|publisher=Zero Tackle|date=|access-date=8 March 2015|archive-date=14 October 2014|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20141014081012/http://www.zerotackle.com/nrl/samao-train-on-squad-announced-14837/|url-status=dead}}</ref> He was subsequently selected in the Samoan final 24 man squad.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/toa-samoa-name-four-nations-squad/tabid/10874/newsid/82434/default.aspx|title=Toa Samoa name Four Nations squad|publisher=NRL.com|date=7 October 2014|access-date=8 March 2015}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.zerotackle.com/nrl/toa-samoa-name-four-nations-squad-15250/|title=Toa Samoa name Four Nations squad|publisher=Zero Tackle|access-date=8 March 2015|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160203031215/http://www.zerotackle.com/nrl/toa-samoa-name-four-nations-squad-15250/|archive-date=3 February 2016|df=dmy-all}}</ref> On 23 October, Sue alongside Samoa teammates [[Reni Maitua]] and [[Tautau Moga]] were fined $10,000 for their involvement in a brawl in at a nightclub at [[Fortitude Valley]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://au.news.yahoo.com/nrl-stars-in-court-over-nightclub-fight-25418742.html|title=NRL stars in court over nightclub fight|website=au.news.yahoo.com}}</ref>
The trio were dropped for 2 matches but later returned to play against New Zealand in Samoa's 16-12 loss at [[Toll Stadium]] in [[Whangarei, New Zealand]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.skysports.com/rugbyleague/match_report/0,,11070_3730177,00.html|title=Match Report; New Zealand v Samoa - 1st November 2014|publisher=Sky Sports|date=|access-date=8 March 2015}}</ref> Sue later said, "It was the worst experience I had ever gone through to that point. There’s a little scuffle, a push and shove outside the club and Reni and I were walking past together. The coppers were trying to sort it out and they were pushing everyone around. Then a copper tackled us from behind and before we knew it we were locked up in Brisbane."<ref>{{cite web| work= Fox Sports | title=Rep Round: Samoan prop Sauaso Sue recalls night in Brisbane lockup during 2014 Four Nations| author=Dan Walsh | date=5 May 2017| url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/teams/tigers/rep-round-samoan-prop-sauaso-sue-recalls-night-in-brisbane-lockup-during-2014-four-nations/news-story/ba4550f1805453d90b0632417b93e8f0|archive-date=9 June 2017|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170609123158/https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/teams/tigers/rep-round-samoan-prop-sauaso-sue-recalls-night-in-brisbane-lockup-during-2014-four-nations/news-story/ba4550f1805453d90b0632417b93e8f0}}</ref>

===2015===
On 23 January 2015, Sue was named in the Wests Tigers [[2015 Auckland Nines]] squad.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/wests-tigers-2015-nines-squad/|title=WESTS TIGERS NAME 2015 NINES SQUAD|publisher=Rugby League Week|date=23 January 2015|access-date=8 March 2015}}</ref> On 2 May 2015, Sue played for Samoa in the 2015 [[Polynesian Cup]]. He played a personal best 23 games for the Wests Tigers, only missing one game due to a shoulder injury, and had stints starting at both prop and as a second-rower.<ref>{{cite web| work= weststigers.com.au| title=2015 Season Review: Sauaso Sue| author=Alicia Newton and Dan Talintyre| url=http://www.weststigers.com.au/news/2015/10/14/_2015_season_review_.html}}</ref>

===2016===
In February, Sue played for the Tigers in the [[2016 NRL Auckland Nines]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/teams/tigers/tigers-place-stars-on-ice-for-nrl-auckland-nines-campaign/news-story/87a0d4ba1b64785792c369f46510e4a0|title=Tigers place stars on ice for NRL Auckland Nines campaign|work=Fox Sports|author=Tim Williams|access-date=2016-03-06}}</ref> On 7 May, he played for Samoa against [[Tonga national rugby league team|Tonga]] in the 2016 [[Polynesian Cup]],<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/samoa-too-good-for-tonga-in-fiery-pacific-test-clash-at-pirtek-stadium/news-story/aa55d4e66b709a32a0637457807f329e|title=Samoa too good for Tonga in fiery Pacific Test clash at Pirtek Stadium|publisher=Dailytelegraph.com.au|date=2016-05-07|access-date=2016-05-08}}</ref> and represented them again in a [[2016 Samoa vs. Fiji rugby league test match|test]] against [[Fiji national rugby league team|Fiji]] in October.<ref>{{cite web| work= Fox Sports| title=Match Centre| url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/match?matchid=RLI20160201}}</ref> For Wests Tigers, Sue began the season playing at lock. By the end of the season, he was starting in the front row with mid-season transfer [[Elijah Taylor (rugby league)|Elijah Taylor]] playing at lock. In round 25, Sue scored his first ever double in a game against the Warriors, taking his season total to a personal-best 4 tries from 21 matches.<ref>{{cite web| work= Stuff | title=Warriors lose to Wests Tigers to end finals hopes| author=David Long | date=28 August 2016| url=https://www.stuff.co.nz/sport/league/83637675/warriors-lose-to-wests-tigers-to-end-finals-hopes}}</ref>

===2017===
Missing 2 games through suspension, Sue played 24 games for the season, scoring 4 tries. At season's end, he declined the chance to play in the World Cup to get married.<ref>{{cite web| work= Daily Telegraph| title=Wests Tigers survivor Sauaso Sue says nothing but love for new crew| author=Christian Nicolussi | url=https://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/wests-tigers-survivor-sauaso-sue-says-nothing-but-love-for-new-crew/news-story/8fbe9fa877344e2b1f31dc7b7508a9d8}}</ref>

===2018===
On 13 August, Sue signed a two-year deal to join Canterbury-Bankstown for the 2019 season.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.sportingnews.com/au/league/news/sauaso-sue-canterbury-bulldogs-nrl-wests-tigers/d2ay3gg2srrv1m7980pyn0h7f|title=Sauaso Sue leaves Wests Tigers behind for new life at Canterbury Bulldogs|website=www.sportingnews.com|date=13 August 2018 }}</ref>

===2019===
Sue made his debut for Canterbury-Bankstown against the [[New Zealand Warriors]] in Round 1 which ended in a 40-6 defeat.  Following a Round 2 loss against rivals Parramatta which ended in another heavy defeat, Sue was demoted to reserve grade by coach [[Dean Pay]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2019/03/31/pay-day-as-contract-extension-propels-players-to-kick-start-season/|title=Pay day as contract extension propels players to kick-start season|website=www.nrl.com}}</ref>

In Round 5, Sue was recalled to the Canterbury side as the club were defeated 40-4 by St George. Sue scored his first try for Canterbury in Round 11 against Melbourne which ended in a 28-6 defeat at [[Belmore Sports Ground]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.abc.net.au/news/2019-05-26/nrl-bulldogs-storm-dragons-sharks-scorecentre/11146096/|title=Melbourne sweep past Canterbury 28-6, Sharks defeat Dragons 22-9 in NRL round 11|newspaper=ABC News|date=26 May 2019 }}</ref>

In Round 17 against Newcastle, Sue was taken from the field during Canterbury's 20-14 win with an injury.  Scans later revealed that Sue had torn his [[anterior cruciate ligament]] (ACL) and he was ruled out for the rest of the season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://7news.com.au/sport/rugby-league/bulldogs-sue-out-for-rest-of-year-c-268642/|title=Bulldogs' Sue out for rest of year|website=7News|date=15 July 2019 }}</ref>

===2020===
On 22 September, Sue was one of eight players who were told by Canterbury that their services would not be required at the club in 2021.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/teams/bulldogs/nrl-2020-canterburybankstown-bulldogs-announce-eight-player-departures-kieran-foran-aiden-tolman-full-list/news-story/67fbadc55112e80ee342e26861bd206d?fbclid=IwAR0j-ofZNc_yFpjOkCEKeXn5uCy1T8rVDj664RO64BlUqgZQl5547RSu6ZM/|title=Dogs confirm EIGHT-man clean-out in first glimpse of Barrett era|website=www.foxsports.com.au|date=22 September 2020 }}</ref> In November, he signed a 2-year contract with the [[Newcastle Knights]] starting in 2021.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.newcastleherald.com.au/story/7018623/ex-samoan-international-prop-links-with-knights-on-two-year-deal/|title=Ex-Samoan international prop links with Knights on two-year deal|first=BARRY|last=TOOHEY|date=18 November 2020|website=Newcastle Herald}}</ref>

===2021===
In round 13 of the [[2021 NRL season]], Sue was sent to the sin bin for a late tackle on [[Reed Mahoney]] during the club's 40-4 loss against Parramatta.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2021-newcastle-knights-vs-parramatta-eels-live-blog-live-stream-supercoach-scores-videos-updates-reed-mahoney-jake-clifford/news-story/293e7170e956a8a17aa6a60124420d1d/|title=Milestone men Gutho and Sivo shine; Over-keen Knights 'panic' on old boys day: 3 Big Hits|website=www.foxsports.com.au|date=6 June 2021 }}</ref> He made a total of 23 appearances throughout the season for Newcastle including their elimination finals loss to [[Parramatta Eels|Parramatta]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.abc.net.au/news/2021-09-12/nrl-parramatta-eels-defeat-newcastle-knights/100436668/|title=Parramatta Eels defeat Newcastle Knights 28-20 in NRL elimination final|newspaper=ABC News|date=12 September 2021}}</ref>

===2022===
Sue appeared in seven games for Newcastle throughout the season.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/matches/Custom/MTUtMjE0MywyMTgwLDIzNDItLTQtLS0tLTIwMjcxLS0tLS0tLS0tLS0tLS0tLS0tLS0tLS0tLS0tLS0tLS0tLQ==|title=Custom Match List|website=www.rugbyleagueproject.org}}</ref>

In July, Sue signed a three-year contract with English [[Super League]] side [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] starting in 2023.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.superleague.co.uk/article/2923/robins-capture-sauaso-sue|title=Robins capture Sauaso Sue|website=www.superleague.co.uk}}</ref>

===2023===
Sue made his club debut for Hull Kingston Rovers in round 1 of the 2023 Super League season as they upset [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] 27-18.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/64668447|title=Hull KR 27-18 Wigan: Shaun Kenny-Dowall scores first Super League hat-trick in Rovers win|work=BBC Sport }}</ref>
Sue played 14 games for Hull Kingston Rovers in the 2023 Super League season as the club finished fourth on the table and qualified for the playoffs.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/live/rugby-league/67008107|title=Relive Wigan's thumping win v Hull KR to head to Grand Final|work=BBC Sport |date=6 October 2023 }}</ref>

===2024===
On 12 October, Sue played in Hull Kingston Rovers [[2024 Super League Grand Final]] loss against Wigan.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/live/2024/oct/12/wigan-v-hull-kr-super-league-grand-final-2024-live-score-updates?page=with:block-670aaacb8f087ed1d5a8a350/|title=Wigan v Hull KR: Super League Grand Final 2024 – as it happened|website=www.theguardian.com}}</ref>

===2025===
On 7 June, Sue played in Hull Kingston Rovers 8-6 Challenge Cup final victory over Warrington. It was the clubs first major trophy in 40 years.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/articles/cn0gnqllpxlo|title=In pictures: Hull KR Challenge Cup victory parade|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>
On 10 June, Sue was found not guilty of a spitting charge during Hull Kingston Rovers Challenge Cup final victory. Sue would have been suspended for six matches if found guilty.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/sauaso-sue-learns-spitting-allegation-10254719/|title=Sauaso Sue learns spitting allegation fate after Challenge Cup Final tribunal|website=www.hulldailymail.com}}</ref>
On 18 September, Sue played in Hull Kingston Rovers victory over Warrington in the last game of the season were the club lifted the League Leaders Shield <ref>https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c39rzxxpyjeo</ref>
On 9 October, Sue played in Hull Kingston Rovers 2025 Super League Grand Final victory over Wigan.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cx2jw497jpzo/|title=Hull KR beat Wigan in Grand Final to complete treble|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>

===2026===
On 19 February, Sue played in Hull Kingston Rovers [[World Club Challenge]] victory against [[Brisbane Broncos|Brisbane]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/live/cwy6dlgx3kyt/|title=Relive Hull KR's World Club Challenge win over Brisbane|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>
